text.title: PMBoK 8 Five Focus Areas
text.summary: Five temporal focus areas replace Process Groups; same structure applies to predictive and agile with different artefacts.

list.item: Initiating — establish vision, purpose, alignment. Predictive: project charter. Agile: vision, goals.
Planning — define approach, build estimates, identify risks. Predictive: WBS, requirements. Agile: backlog, user stories.
Executing — perform work to achieve objectives. Predictive: work packages. Agile: sprints, iterations.
Monitoring and Controlling — track performance, manage changes. Predictive: EVM, baselines. Agile: velocity, burndown.
Closing — formal completion, lessons learned. Predictive: final reports. Agile: project-end retrospective.

section.title: Focus Areas are Aspects, Not Phases

rule.statement: Treat focus areas as aspects of ongoing work, not sequential phases; Planning and Monitoring and Controlling occur throughout the project lifecycle, not just once.
rule.rationale: Conflating focus areas with phases re-introduces waterfall thinking and misrepresents how agile and hybrid projects operate.

section.title: 40 Non-Prescriptive Processes
section.p: PMBoK 8 distributes 40 processes across the 5 focus areas and 7 performance domains. They describe how work is commonly performed — not a mandatory checklist. Select the subset with measurable inputs and outputs for your context; skip the rest explicitly.

rule.statement: Do not apply all 40 processes by default; document which processes are adopted and which are tailored out, with justification.
rule.rationale: Applying the full menu creates overhead that exceeds value, especially for small or agile projects.

section.title: 5 x 7 Matrix
section.p: Use focus areas as the temporal index (when) and the seven performance domains as the topical index (what). The project plan is a 5 x 7 matrix; fill only the cells that apply. Empty cells are valid and must be explicitly null — do not invent artefacts to fill them.
section.reference: Matrix YAML to stage-gate checklist generator
